features: CFG mode control: hmac_en, sha_en, endian_swap, digest_swap
STATUS reporting: fifo_full, fifo_empty, fifo_depth, sha_idle
Message FIFO depth and backpressure behavior
Digest register readout and byte-order handling

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_and_csr_map
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values, TL-UL read/write access to all implemented CSRs, reserved-bit read-as-zero behavior, and unmapped address error response.

directed_tests.name: cfg_mode_sanity
directed_tests.intent: Program valid CFG combinations for bare SHA and HMAC modes, confirm illegal hmac_en without sha_en is rejected or safely ignored per RTL behavior, and check status reflects idle/empty after configuration.

directed_tests.name: bare_sha_single_block_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Run a single-block SHA-256 test with a known message and compare digest registers against a software reference model, including digest_swap and endian_swap permutations.

directed_tests.name: bare_sha_multi_block_streaming
directed_tests.intent: Exercise hash_start, repeated message writes, hash_process, and hash_stop across multiple 512-bit blocks to verify streaming accumulation and final digest correctness.

directed_tests.name: hmac_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known 256-bit key and message, execute full HMAC flow, and compare final digest against a software HMAC-SHA256 reference.

directed_tests.name: fifo_depth_and_full_empty
directed_tests.intent: Fill the 32-entry FIFO to observe fifo_depth, fifo_full, and fifo_empty transitions, then drain through processing to confirm status updates and no data loss.

directed_tests.name: wipe_secret_zeroization
directed_tests.intent: After loading key material and/or completing an HMAC operation, write WIPE_SECRET and verify key registers and secret-related state are cleared while non-secret control/status behavior remains functional.

directed_tests.name: reset_during_active_operation
directed_tests.intent: Assert reset while a hash operation is in progress and confirm all state, FIFO contents, status bits, and digest outputs return to reset values without bus lockup.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_and_alert_smoke
directed_tests.intent: Check that interrupt outputs and alert_o remain quiescent in nominal flows and only assert if the RTL exposes completion/error signaling; otherwise confirm stable inactive behavior.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_smoke
random_tests.intent: Randomize legal TL-UL reads and writes across the CSR map, including reserved bits and repeated accesses, to catch decode, masking, and side-effect issues within the time budget.

random_tests.name: random_message_streaming_sha
random_tests.intent: Generate random message lengths, chunk sizes, and command interleavings for bare SHA mode, then compare digest results to a Python reference model.

random_tests.name: random_message_streaming_hmac
random_tests.intent: Generate random keys and messages for HMAC mode with varied chunking and finalization timing, checking digest correctness against a software reference.

random_tests.name: random_endian_digest_swap_matrix
random_tests.intent: Randomly sweep endian_swap and digest_swap combinations across representative messages to validate byte-order transformations and register packing.

random_tests.name: fifo_pressure_random
random_tests.intent: Apply randomized write bursts and processing commands to stress FIFO occupancy, backpressure, and status coherence without exceeding the 60-minute execution budget.

risk_areas.reason: W1S command registers are prone to edge-trigger and race-condition bugs, especially around hash_start/hash_process/hash_stop ordering and repeated writes.

risk_areas.area: HMAC key expansion and double-pass finalization
risk_areas.reason: HMAC introduces extra internal state, ipad/opad processing, and automatic outer hash sequencing, which are common sources of functional mismatch.
SHA-256 finalization with arbitrary message lengths is error-prone, especially when the FIFO contains partial blocks at hash_stop.
The 32-entry FIFO and status bits must remain consistent under bursty writes and processing; off-by-one errors are likely.
endian_swap and digest_swap can silently corrupt data paths if word/byte ordering is implemented inconsistently.
Secret clearing must remove key material and internal state without breaking subsequent operations; scope ambiguity increases risk.
Unmapped address handling and reserved-bit masking are straightforward but important for bus robustness.
These outputs may be lightly implemented or unused; smoke coverage is sufficient within the time budget.
